@Pushpal Das Reason for Delay:
 The slot timing calculations on QS222 were implemented incorrectly in the script. We received the official documentation for slot timing calculations yesterday, and we are currently applying it to the script. We expect this update to resolve the previously reported issues.

comments.text:
Current Problems observed in the first debug cycle.
Problem #1(Fixed)
Unable to run tests for packet sizes beyond 1792 bytes.​
Root cause: On FW max_buffer_size was set to 1800, which limited usable packet size to 1792 bytes(excluding header).​
Fix: Increased max_buffer_size to 1814 bytes, allowing previously failing test cases to run successfully​
Problem #2
RS + CRC enabled, getting unexpected results​
Complete description of the issue : 61597?block=block-0b42d49d-a301-4679-977d-e4f5e8db6414
Problem #3​
DR_Scale > 2, QAM4,QAM16,QAM64 not working.​
QAM2 1800 Packet Size Higher DR_Scale not working.​

• Bring out the signal from internals as well as internal modules to see what is happening there. ~Vinayak Agrawal

• Utilize the available QAM 16 data to improve EVM results. ~Vinayak Agrawal
 
• Run 100 for the PDFP experiments as suggested and then move over to COM 16. ~Vinayak Agrawal
 
• Sample INQ data using SRAM with the symbol clock.

tasks.name: N3: [RX validation] Debug the PMDR floor for data rate < 5Mbps without idle slots.
tasks.description:
Debug Results PPT: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX\_Testing/RX\_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=b5gIsb&nav=eyJzSWQiOjcyNSwiY0lkIjoxMDQ5NTkwNzY2fQ](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX_Testing/RX_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=b5gIsb&nav=eyJzSWQiOjcyNSwiY0lkIjoxMDQ5NTkwNzY2fQ)

This issue looks like a f/w issue.
With packet interval = Active slot, we see two problems
1. We only see 650 number of IRQs out of 1000. By increasing the packet interval (i.e. adding idle slot), we do not see this problem and the IRQs are as expected.
2. The SPI readout is not happening randomly even though the IRQ was provided by the chip.

These issues are to be checked in the f/w and Kumaresh will take this up.

Please find the scope captures :

IRQ received only 650 times out of 1000:
![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/fe2fd62a-9476-4a0d-95ea-07ad9dc880dd/image.png)

SPI readout not happening even though IRQ is present:
![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/9fb8f69c-280d-465c-a0ed-b0e7d3aab0fb/image.png)

Resolution:
The issue was with the software where the wait time for 'test done' was being calculated in terms of the duration set in the GUI as below. 

wait\_for\_test\_done = duration \* pkt\_count + 5

So when we set the duration as 0 in the GUI for avoiding the idle slot, the wait time is 5sec (= 0 \* 1000 + 5). This time is not sufficient for lower data rates and hence the test was stopped before receiving all packets. 

We tested with the corrected code which takes in the IC interval (active slot + idle slot) instead of GUI duration setting. This resolved the issue. 

wait\_for\_test\_done = **IC interval** \* pkt\_count + 5

comments.author: Sarat Anumula

The issue was with the software where the wait time for 'test done' was being calculated in terms of the duration set in the GUI as below. 

wait\_for\_test\_done = duration \* pkt\_count + 5

So when we set the duration as 0 in the GUI for avoiding the idle slot, the wait time is 5sec (= 0 \* 1000 + 5). This time is not sufficient for lower data rates and hence the test was stopped before receiving all packets. 

We tested with the corrected code which takes in the IC interval (active slot + idle slot) instead of GUI duration setting. This resolved the issue. 

wait\_for\_test\_done = **IC interval** \* pkt\_count + 5

tasks.name: N3: [RX validation] PER_FG bringup in schedule mode.
tasks.description:
**Why:** QS 222 needs to be up and running in the schedule mode - this is the POR for the product deliverable. This is the most important test that we need to have it up and running for the QS222 for RX validation.

**Description:**

**Deliverable:** Gather the feedback from the QS222 RX verification (operation) when run in the schedule mode. Metrics is the just packet reception (no EVM , no PMDR) -- an indication of Silicon being alive. Idea is to have this packet reception working on one device at one temp.

**Limitations:**
(D) Need design , Firmware, help from Sayantan , Souptik, Kumaresh to debug any issues surface during the Rx validation.

comments.text: The test is up and running. We had hoped the test would solve a bug, but it didn't. So we are working on that debug right now
